methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Ares Industries is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Alex Tseng and Devan Plantamura, currently valued at $150M post-money on $31M raised to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private players in the same sector and stage cohort, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the current $150M post-money valuation, implying $300M. Peer-median analysis across the 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $60M to $1.5B. The peer cohort is dominated by Series B rounds; Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI command valuations at or above $1.4B.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $255M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median estimate of $650M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around Ares' competitive positioning relative to better-capitalized Series B peers. Key variables affecting placement within this band include revenue traction, customer concentration in government versus commercial channels, and the company's path to Series B pricing.
